questions.text: I find that I can maintain equanimity even when those around me are emotional.
I believe that approaching others' pain with structured reasoning allows me to be more consistently helpful over time.
I believe that a well-developed sense of personal ambition is attractive and admirable.
I find deep contentment in giving away things I worked hard to earn.
I wake up some mornings already buzzing with emotional energy before anything has happened.
I hold the view that the moral concern I feel for suffering people is the experience that most directly informs my sense of what a good life requires.
I rarely notice when someone downplays their accomplishments for social reasons.
I often help synthesize multiple perspectives into a direction the group can support.
I tend to be focused on my own train of thought during conversations rather than scanning for reactions.
What some call procrastination, I experience as waiting for the right moment of clarity.
I find myself correcting course when I think a group is going the wrong way.
I find that I am better positioned to help when I can evaluate a situation without being influenced by the emotions it generates in me.
I frequently take on supporting or facilitating roles in team settings.
I feel that enjoying the fruits of my own labor is exactly what hard work is for.
I find that not knowing what comes next is exciting rather than stressful.
I review my calendar at the start of each day to set my intentions.
I feel that my inner life runs at a steady, sustainable pace.
I often catch the quick glance two people exchange when something significant is said.
